title: Spicy Crispy Egyptian Fava-Beans Falafel
description: A vegan-friendly twist on the classic Egyptian fava bean falafel, infused with rose water and spicy kick.
instructions: Soak the fava beans in water for at least 8 hours or overnight. Drain and rinse.
In a blender or food processor, combine fava beans, bulgur wheat, onion, garlic, parsley, rose water, lemon juice, salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper. Blend until well combined.
Using your hands, shape the mixture into small patties. You should end up with around 12-15 falafel.
In a shallow dish, mix together cornstarch and a pinch of salt.
Dip each falafel into the cornstarch mixture, coating both sides evenly.
Heat about 1-2 inches of vegan oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. When the oil is hot, add a few falafel to the pan (do not overcrowd).
Fry the falafel for about 3-4 minutes on each side, until they're golden brown and crispy.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the falafel from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the falafel hot with your favorite dipping sauce or in a pita with your favorite toppings.
